a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Poverty: Share of population living on less than $3.65 a day, 2023
The poverty line of $3.65 per day is set by the World Bank to be representative of the definitions of poverty
adopted in lower-middle-income countries. This data is adjusted for inflation and for differences in living costs
Data source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform (2024)

Note: This data is expressed in
international-$
at 2017 prices. Depending on the country and year, it relates to income measured after taxes

and benefits, or to consumption,
per capita
.
